# Lockfile & Version Pinning

## The lockfile

`vanic add` writes a `vani.lock` file next to your `vani.toml`. This file
records the exact version and checksum of every dependency (direct and
transitive) that was resolved.

```toml
# vani.lock — generated by vanic, do not edit by hand
[[package]]
name    = "calculus"
version = "0.1.0"
cksum   = "4131d2cd1194705c0f89191bc79068cd6c087d0c12180e5872f968d3ce626a3e"
source  = "kosh"
```

**Commit `vani.lock` to version control.** This guarantees every developer
and every CI run builds with the same package versions.

---

## Version range syntax

Kosh uses standard [SemVer](https://semver.org/) ranges:

| Specifier | Meaning | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `^1.2.3` | Compatible: `>=1.2.3, <2.0.0` | `^0.1` → `>=0.1.0, <0.2.0` |
| `~1.2.3` | Patch-level: `>=1.2.3, <1.3.0` | |
| `=1.2.3` | Exact version only | |
| `*` | Any version (not recommended) | |

> **Note:** For packages with a `0.x` major version, `^0.1` only allows
> patch-level updates (`>=0.1.0, <0.2.0`). Minor bumps in `0.x` may be
> breaking.

---

## Updating dependencies

```
vanic update             # update all deps to newest compatible version
vanic update calculus    # update only calculus
```

After updating, inspect the diff in `vani.lock` and re-run your tests before
committing.

---

## Yanked versions

If a package author yanks a version (marks `"yanked": true` in the index),
`vanic add` will skip it automatically. An existing lockfile that pins a yanked
version will produce a warning at build time but will still compile — the
checksum is still verifiable from the cached tarball.
